methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 20 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Roboneers is a Growth-stage company in Defense & Security that has raised $2M+. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 10 private and 2 public companies in the sector, including peers such as Anduril Industries, Shield AI, and Flock Safety, though no direct same-stage comparables with sufficient scale exist to anchor a peer-median approach.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable here. Using the $2M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Growth-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $2.4M. The Peer-Median method does not apply; while 20 same-sector companies exist, filtering for stage, adjacency, and valuation tier yielded fewer than three comparable datapoints, making a narrow peer median unreliable. A broad all-sector median would introduce material noise and is therefore excluded.

The triangulated valuation range is $2.0M (low) to $2.76M (high), with a median of $2.4M. This range reflects a single applicable method; the low end incorporates a modest 2% buffer, and the high end applies a 15% step-up variance. Key uncertainties include the absence of tight peer anchors at this stage and valuation tier, the reliance on raised capital as a proxy for current value, and limited visibility into Roboneers' revenue, customer concentration, and technology differentiation relative to the named peer set.
